description: Manga Reading Tracker - A web app to track manga reading progress and receive email notifications when new chapters are released

userStories.title: Create manga table migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the manga table in PostgreSQL so the app can persist tracked manga.
acceptanceCriteria: Flyway migration V1__create_manga_table.sql creates manga table with columns: id (UUID PK), title (VARCHAR 255 NOT NULL), source_url (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE), current_chapter (INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0), latest_chapter (INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0), notifications_enabled (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE), last_checked_at (TIMESTAMP NULLABLE), created_at (TIMESTAMP NOT NULL), updated_at (TIMESTAMP NOT NULL)
Unique index on manga(source_url)
Migration runs successfully on app startup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create notification_log and app_settings table migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the notification_log and app_settings tables so the app can track sent notifications and persist settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Flyway migration V2__create_notification_log_table.sql creates notification_log table with columns: id (UUID PK), manga_id (UUID FK → manga.id), chapter_number (INTEGER NOT NULL), sent_at (TIMESTAMP NOT NULL)
Unique index on notification_log(manga_id, chapter_number) to prevent duplicate sends

Flyway migration V3__create_app_settings_table.sql creates app_settings table with columns: id (INTEGER PK DEFAULT 1), email_notifications_enabled (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE), notification_email (VARCHAR 255 NOT NULL), poll_interval_minutes (INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 30)
CHECK constraint on app_settings ensures id = 1 (single-row table)
Seed row inserted for app_settings on migration (id=1, email='user@localhost', poll=30)
Migrations run successfully on app startup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MangaScraper interface, ScraperRegistry, and SakuraMangasScraper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a modular scraper layer so the app can extract manga title and latest chapter from sakuramangas.org.
acceptanceCriteria: MangaScraper interface defined with methods: boolean supports(String url) and ScrapedManga scrape(String url) throws ScrapingException
ScrapedManga record defined with fields: String title, int latestChapter
ScrapingException defined as a runtime exception
ScraperRegistry @Component resolves the correct MangaScraper for a given URL; throws UnsupportedSourceException if none found
SakuraMangasScraper @Component implements MangaScraper for sakuramangas.org URLs
SakuraMangasScraper uses Jsoup to fetch the page and extracts title and latest chapter number
SakuraMangasScraper throws ScrapingException if title or chapter cannot be extracted
Network timeout retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff
Jsoup dependency added to pom.xml
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MangaService: add, list, update, and delete manga
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MangaService to orchestrate all business logic for managing tracked manga.
acceptanceCriteria: Manga JPA entity and MangaRepository (JpaRepository) created

MangaService.addManga(String sourceUrl) validates URL against supported sites, calls ScraperRegistry to scrape, saves to DB with currentChapter=0; throws DuplicateMangaException if sourceUrl already exists
MangaService.listManga() returns all manga sorted by updatedAt DESC

MangaService.updateManga(UUID id, Integer currentChapter, Boolean notificationsEnabled) updates allowed fields; validates currentChapter >= 0 and <= latestChapter; throws MangaNotFoundException if id not found
MangaService.deleteManga(UUID id) removes manga and cascades to its notification_log entries; throws MangaNotFoundException if id not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SettingsService and AppSettings repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SettingsService to read and write the single-row app_settings table.
acceptanceCriteria: AppSettings JPA entity and AppSettingsRepository (JpaRepository) created
SettingsService.getSettings() returns the single app_settings row (id=1); creates default row if missing
SettingsService.updateSettings(Boolean emailNotificationsEnabled, String notificationEmail, Integer pollIntervalMinutes) updates and saves the row
notificationEmail validated as a non-blank string
pollIntervalMinutes validated as a positive integer
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement NotificationService: detect new chapters and send emails
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the NotificationService to detect new chapters for a manga and send deduplicated email notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: NotificationLog JPA entity and NotificationLogRepository created
NotificationService.notify(Manga manga, int newLatestChapter) checks if notification already sent for that chapter via notification_log unique constraint; skips if already sent
Email sent only if manga.notificationsEnabled = true AND app_settings.emailNotificationsEnabled = true AND newLatestChapter > manga.currentChapter
Email includes: manga title, new chapter number, link to source URL
Email sent using Spring JavaMailSender; from address from app.notification.from-email config
NotificationLog entry saved after successful send to prevent duplicates
Spring Mail dependency added to pom.xml; Mailhog configured in application.properties for local dev
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement periodic scraping job (@Scheduled)
userStories.description: As a system, I need a background job that periodically polls all tracked manga URLs for new chapter releases.
acceptanceCriteria: ScrapingJob @Component with @Scheduled method runs at configurable interval (default 30 minutes) using app.scraper.poll-interval config
For each manga in the DB, calls ScraperRegistry to scrape the sourceUrl
If latestChapter from scrape > current manga.latestChapter, updates manga.latestChapter and manga.updatedAt in DB, then calls NotificationService.notify()
Scraping failures (network timeout, parse error, 4xx/5xx) are caught and logged per manga; job continues to remaining manga
manga.lastCheckedAt updated after each attempt (success or failure)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement MangaController REST endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the REST API endpoints for manga CRUD so the Angular frontend can communicate with the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/manga returns 200 with JSON array of all manga sorted by updatedAt DESC
POST /api/manga accepts { sourceUrl } body, returns 201 with created manga JSON; returns 400 for unsupported/malformed URL, 422 if scraping cannot extract data, 409 if URL already tracked
PATCH /api/manga/{id} accepts { currentChapter?, notificationsEnabled? } body, returns 200 with updated manga JSON; returns 404 if not found, 400 if currentChapter validation fails
DELETE /api/manga/{id} returns 204 No Content; returns 404 if not found
All responses use application/json
MangaController annotated with @RestController and @RequestMapping('/api/manga')
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SettingsController REST endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the REST API endpoints for reading and updating app settings.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/settings returns 200 with current app settings JSON
PUT /api/settings accepts { emailNotificationsEnabled, notificationEmail, pollIntervalMinutes } body, returns 200 with updated settings JSON; returns 400 on validation failure
SettingsController annotated with @RestController and @RequestMapping('/api/settings')
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Angular HTTP service for manga and settings API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Angular service that wraps all API calls so components can interact with the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: MangaService Angular service created with methods: getManga(), addManga(sourceUrl), updateManga(id, patch), deleteManga(id)
SettingsService Angular service created with methods: getSettings(), updateSettings(settings)
All methods return Observable using HttpClient
HttpClientModule (or provideHttpClient()) configured in app.config or app.module
API base URL configurable via environment.ts (default: http://localhost:8080)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build reading list dashboard component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my tracked manga in a list so I can manage my reading progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard component displays all tracked manga in a list/table
Each row shows: title, current chapter, latest available chapter, source URL (as link)
Manga with unread chapters (latestChapter > currentChapter) are visually distinguished with a badge or highlight
List is sorted by most recently updated (backend returns this order)
Dashboard loads manga on init via MangaService.getManga()
Loading state shown while fetching
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build add-manga form component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to paste a manga URL so the system automatically detects the title and adds it to my list.
acceptanceCriteria: Input field accepts a URL string
Submit button calls MangaService.addManga(url) and shows loading state
On success: shows confirmation with detected manga title and refreshes the reading list
On error 400/409/422: shows descriptive error message to user
Input field cleared after successful add
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build update-reading-progress UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to update which chapter I've read so the tracker reflects my actual progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Each manga row has an editable current chapter field (input or +/- buttons)
Input validated client-side: must be a positive integer, cannot exceed latestChapter
On change, calls MangaService.updateManga(id, { currentChapter }) and updates row immediately
Error shown if server rejects the update
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build delete-manga UI with confirmation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to remove a manga I no longer follow so my list stays clean.
acceptanceCriteria: Each manga row has a delete button
Clicking delete shows a confirmation dialog/prompt before deletion
On confirm, calls MangaService.deleteManga(id) and removes the manga from the list
On cancel, no action taken
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build notification preferences settings page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enable or disable email notifications so I control what I receive.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page accessible via navigation link
Global toggle to enable/disable all email notifications (emailNotificationsEnabled)
Input field for notification email address
Poll interval input (minutes)
Save button calls SettingsService.updateSettings() and shows success/error feedback
Per-manga notification toggle in the reading list calls MangaService.updateManga(id, { notificationsEnabled }) and updates immediately
Settings page loads current settings on init via SettingsService.getSettings()
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Write unit tests for scraper layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the scraper logic so HTML parsing and data extraction are verified automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests written BEFORE or alongside implementation (TDD)
Tests for SakuraMangasScraper.supports(): returns true for sakuramangas.org URLs, false for others
Tests for SakuraMangasScraper.scrape(): given sample HTML, correctly extracts title and latestChapter
Tests for SakuraMangasScraper.scrape(): throws ScrapingException when title or chapter cannot be extracted
Tests for ScraperRegistry.resolve(): returns correct scraper for known URL, throws UnsupportedSourceException for unknown URL
Tests use JUnit 5 + Mockito; Jsoup is tested with stubbed HTML strings
All tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write unit tests for service layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for MangaService, NotificationService, and SettingsService so core business logic is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests written BEFORE or alongside implementation (TDD)
MangaService tests: addManga() happy path saves manga with correct fields; throws DuplicateMangaException on duplicate URL; throws UnsupportedSourceException for bad URL
MangaService tests: updateManga() validates currentChapter bounds; throws MangaNotFoundException for unknown id
MangaService tests: deleteManga() removes entity; throws MangaNotFoundException for unknown id
NotificationService tests: notify() skips send if notification already in log; skips if notificationsEnabled = false; saves log entry after send
SettingsService tests: getSettings() creates default row if missing; updateSettings() validates and saves
Tests use JUnit 5 + Mockito; repositories and mail sender are mocked
All tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write integration tests for REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the REST API so endpoint contracts are verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests use Spring Boot @WebMvcTest or @SpringBootTest test slices
POST /api/manga: 201 on valid URL, 400 on unsupported URL, 409 on duplicate URL
GET /api/manga: 200 with correct manga list
PATCH /api/manga/{id}: 200 on valid update, 400 on invalid currentChapter, 404 on unknown id
DELETE /api/manga/{id}: 204 on success, 404 on unknown id
GET /api/settings and PUT /api/settings: 200 on success, 400 on invalid input
ScraperService mocked in API tests to avoid real HTTP calls
All tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write integration tests for repository layer with Testcontainers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want repository integration tests backed by a real PostgreSQL container so data layer is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Testcontainers dependency added to pom.xml (testcontainers-postgresql)
MangaRepository tests: save, findAll sorted by updatedAt DESC, findBySourceUrl, delete
NotificationLogRepository tests: save notification log entry; unique constraint prevents duplicate (manga_id, chapter_number)
AppSettingsRepository tests: save and retrieve single settings row
Tests use @DataJpaTest with a Testcontainers PostgreSQL instance
All tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up Playwright and write E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want end-to-end tests with Playwright so critical user flows are verified in a real browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Playwright installed and configured for the Angular frontend (playwright.config.ts)
E2E test: add manga by URL → verify it appears in the reading list with detected title
E2E test: update reading progress → verify the chapter number updates in the UI
E2E test: remove manga → verify it is gone from the list after confirmation
Tests run against the locally running frontend (localhost:4200) with backend at localhost:8080
All E2E tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Dockerize backend with multi-stage Dockerfile
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Docker image for the Spring Boot backend so it can be built and run consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile in backend/ (or root) with multi-stage build: stage 1 uses Maven to compile and package; stage 2 uses JRE base image and copies the JAR
Image exposes port 8080
ENTRYPOINT runs the Spring Boot JAR
Build args or ENV vars accepted for: DB_URL, DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD, MAIL_HOST, MAIL_PORT, NOTIFICATION_FROM_EMAIL
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Dockerize frontend with nginx and create docker-compose.yml
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Docker image for the Angular frontend and a docker-compose.yml to run the full stack locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile in frontend/ with multi-stage build: stage 1 uses Node to run ng build; stage 2 uses nginx to serve the dist/ output
nginx.conf proxies /api requests to the backend container
docker-compose.yml defines services: db (PostgreSQL 16), mailhog (SMTP 1025, UI 8025), backend (port 8080, depends_on db), frontend (port 4200, depends_on backend)
docker compose up starts the full stack and the app is reachable at localhost:4200
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configure GitHub Actions CI: format, lint, and test stages
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CI pipeline that enforces code quality and runs all tests on every push and PR.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml created and triggers on push/PR to main
Stage 1 — Format & Lint: Checkstyle fails build on Java violations; ESLint + Prettier fails build on Angular violations; ng build --configuration production passes with strict mode
Stage 2 — Tests: runs JUnit 5 unit tests; runs Spring Boot integration tests; runs Playwright E2E tests
Pipeline fails fast if any step fails
Testcontainers works in CI (Docker-in-Docker or service containers)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configure SonarCloud static analysis stage and Docker build/deploy stage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SonarCloud quality gates and automated Docker image builds in the CI pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: SonarCloud analysis step added to CI workflow after tests
Quality gate configured: 80%+ code coverage, zero bugs, zero code smells; pipeline blocks merge if gate fails
CI stage builds Docker images for backend and frontend
Images pushed to a container registry (GitHub Container Registry or Docker Hub) on merge to main
AI-powered PR review step added (Claude or CodeRabbit action posts review comments on PRs)
Branch protection rule requires all CI stages to pass + at least 1 human approval before merge
Typecheck passes
